paper_title: On classes of graphs with strongly sublinear separators
summary: No published paper resolving this conjecture was found. Dvořák's 2022 Journal of Graph Theory paper 'On weighted sublinear separators' is a likely follow-up, but its full content is behind a paywall and could not be verified. The conjecture that the polylogarithmic-in-n bound on |M| in the weighted balanced separator C∪M can be replaced by a constant depending only on the graph class and ℓ remains, as far as can be determined from open-access sources, open.
search_queries: Dvořák "strongly sublinear separators" balanced separator constant size 1710.03117
graph "balanced separator" "constant size" "polynomial expansion" "omega-expansion" separator conjecture
Dvořák "weighted sublinear separators" 2022 "constant size" conjecture separator
notes: Dvořák published a follow-up paper 'On weighted sublinear separators' in J. Graph Theory 2022 (DOI 10.1002/jgt.22777) which may address this conjecture, but the paper is paywalled and the abstract does not explicitly mention the constant-size M conjecture. The 2022 paper 'Product Structure of Graph Classes with Strongly Sublinear Separators' (arXiv:2208.10074) and the 2026 paper 'Coarse Balanced Separators in Fat-Minor-Free Graphs' (arXiv:2604.11318) do not appear to reference this specific conjecture. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is ~8 years old and a relevant paywalled paper could not be fully verified.
